intro: Matelea squiresii, also known as Squire's Matelea or Squire's Twining Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, characterized by its twining habit and clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, stress, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Matelea squiresii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Matelea squiresii include teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are often used to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
